zh.Boarstall, Buckinghamshire, England: 英國巴金哈姆爾波阿斯塔爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert James於大約1375年出生在英國英格蘭巴金哈姆爾波阿斯塔爾時，他的父親John James26歲，母親Christine Anesty25歲。他於1392年與Catherine de la Pole在英國英格蘭巴金哈姆爾波阿斯塔爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1432年2月16日在英國波克夏瓦林福德享年58歲，埋葬在英國巴金哈姆爾波阿斯塔爾。

zh-hans.Boarstall, Buckinghamshire, England: 英国巴金哈姆爾波阿斯塔爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Robert James于大约1375年出生在英国英格兰巴金哈姆爾波阿斯塔爾时，他的父亲John James26岁，母亲Christine Anesty25岁。他于1392年与Catherine de la Pole在英国英格兰巴金哈姆爾波阿斯塔爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1432年2月16日在英国波克夏瓦林福德享年58岁，埋葬在英国巴金哈姆爾波阿斯塔爾。

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Welsh: from the Middle English personal name James. Introduced to England by the Normans, this is an Old French form of Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Latin Iacobus, Greek Iakōbos, the New Testament rendering of Hebrew Ya‘aqob (see Jacob ). The medieval Latin (Vulgate) Bible distinguished between Old Testament Iacob (which was uninflected) and New Testament Iacobus (with inflections). The latter developed into James in medieval French. The distinction was carried over into the King James Bible of 1611, and Jacob and James remain as separate names in English usage. Most European languages, however, make no such distinction, so that forms such as French Jacques , stand for both the Old and the New Testament names. This surname is also very common among African Americans. Compare Jack .

variants.name: Giacomo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Domenico, Gino, Salvatore.
Italian: from the personal name Giacomo, from Latin Jacobus via Late Latin Jacomus (see James and Jacob ).

variants.name: Jamie
meanings: Scottish (mainly Angus): from a Scots pet form of the personal name   James  .

variants.name: Jemison
meanings: English (northern): patronymic from the personal name Jem (a pet form of James ) + son. Compare Jamison .

variants.name: Jemmett
meanings: English (southern): from the Middle English personal name Jemet, a diminutive of Jem, a pet form of James .

variants.name: Jaime
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Jesus, Jorge, Juan, Manuel, Carlos, Javier, Arturo, Luis, Alfredo, Enrique, Miguel.
Spanish: from the personal name Jaime, an equivalent of James .

variants.name: Jamison
meanings: English and Scottish: from Middle English and Scots Jami(e)son, Jameson, Jem(i)son ‘son of James’ (see James ). Jameson and Jamison are mainly found in northern England, especially in Northumberland and Durham, while in Scotland the more usual form is Jamieson , but all three versions occur on either side of the border.

variants.name: Jacobo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Jesus, Francisco, Juan, Manuel, Miguel, Rafael, Alfredo, Luis, Carlos, Mario, Roberto.
Spanish and Italian: from the Latin personal name Jacobus (see James , Giacomo ).

variants.name: Jemmott
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Alicia, Armando, Dionicio, Gregoria, Guillermo, Roberto, Rosita, Ruperto.
English: from the personal name Jemot, a diminutive of Jem, a pet form of James . This surname is frequent in Barbados, Trinidad and Tobago, and Saint Lucia.

variants.name: Como
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Sal, Salvatore, Giuseppe, Ignazio, Nunzio, Vito, Angelo, Antonio, Camillo, Carlo, Carmela, Caterina.
Italian: from the personal name Como, a short form of Giacomo, an equivalent of James .
Italian: habitational name from Como in Lombardy.

variants.name: Jacob
meanings: Jewish, English, Welsh, German, Portuguese, French (mainly Alsace and Lorraine), Walloon, Breton, Dutch, Flemish, Swedish, Danish, and Norwegian: derivative, via Latin Jacob(us), from the Hebrew personal name Ya‘aqob (Yaakov). In the Bible, this is the name of the crafty younger twin brother of Esau (Genesis 25:26), who took advantage of the latter's hunger and impetuousness to persuade him to part with his birthright ‘for a mess of potage’. The name is traditionally interpreted as coming from Hebrew akev ‘heel’: Jacob is said to have been born holding on to Esau's heel. In English usage the name Jacob is regarded as distinct from the name James , but they are of identical origin. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, for example Assyrian/Chaldean or Arabic Yaqub , Yakub , Yacoub , or Yacob , Slovenian Jakob and Jakop, Czech and Slovak Jakub , and also their patronymics and other derivatives (see examples at Jacobs and Jacobson ). The name Jacob is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Chacko ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert James was born about 1375, in Boarstall, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, John James, was 26 and his mother, Christine Anesty, was 25. He married Catherine de la Pole in 1392, in Boarstall, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 16 February 1432, in Wallingford, Berkshire, England, at the age of 58, and was buried in Boarstall, Buckinghamshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert James est né environ 1375, à Boarstall, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John James, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Christine Anesty, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Catherine de la Pole en 1392, à Boarstall, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 16 février 1432, à Wallingford, Berkshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 58 ans, et a été enterré à Boarstall, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert James um 1375 in Boarstall, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater John James 26 und seine Mutter Christine Anesty 25. Er hat 1392 in Boarstall, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom Catherine de la Pole geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 16. Februar 1432 im Alter von 58 Jahren in Wallingford, Berkshire, England und wurde in Boarstall, Buckinghamshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert James è nato il circa 1375 nel Boarstall, Buckinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, John James, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Christine Anesty, aveva 25 anni. Lui sposato Catherine de la Pole nel 1392, nel Boarstall, Buckinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 16 febbraio 1432, nel Wallingford, Berkshire, England, all'età di 58 anni, e fu sepolto a Boarstall, Buckinghamshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert Jamesが約1375年にイギリスイギリスブクインガムシュアイアボアルスタルで生まれた時，父John Jamesは26歳，母Christine Anestyは25歳であった。1392年にイギリスイギリスブクインガムシュアイアボアルスタルでCatherine de la Poleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1432年2月16日にイギリスバークシュアイアワリングフォードで58歳で死去し，イギリスブクインガムシュアイアボアルスタルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert James(이)가 대략 1375년 영국 잉글랜드 벅잉함실르 보르스탤에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John James(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Christine Anesty(은)는 25세였다. 그는 1392년 영국 잉글랜드 벅잉함실르 보르스탤에서 Catherine de la Pole(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1432년 2월 16일 영국 버크실르 월링퍼드에서 58세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 벅잉함실르 보르스탤에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert James nasceu aproximadamente 1375, em Boarstall, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, John James, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Christine Anesty, tinha 25 anos. Ele casou-se com Catherine de la Pole em 1392, em Boarstall, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 16 de fevereiro de 1432, em Wallingford, Berkshire, Inglaterra, com 58 anos, e foi sepultado em Boarstall, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert James родился около 1375 г. в местности боарсталл, буккингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени John James было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Christine Anesty, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Catherine de la Pole в 1392 г. в местности боарсталл, буккингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 16 февраля 1432 г. в местности валлингфорд, биркшир, Англия в возрасте 58 лет и был похоронен в местности боарсталл, буккингхамшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert James nació aproximadamente 1375, en Boarstall, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, John James, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Christine Anesty, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Catherine de la Pole en 1392, en Boarstall, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 16 de febrero de 1432, en Wallingford, Berkshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 58 años, y fue sepultado en Boarstall, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra.
